title: Japan's Parliament approves controversial reform of the Imperial Household Law
summary: The amendment, approved with the support of the ruling party but without consensus, aims to guarantee the stability of the imperial succession in the face of a shortage of male heirs.

title: A man arrested for abandoning the bodies of two women in the mountains of Nara
summary: Police arrested a 37-year-old suspect after finding the corpses of a mother and daughter, who had been strangled and thrown from a bridge.

title: Torrential rains cause floods and landslides in Tochigi and Gunma prefectures
summary: The overflow of the Hata River forced urgent evacuation orders for thousands of residents, while authorities continue to monitor the risk of further disasters.

title: Japan enacts law penalizing desecration of the national flag
summary: The new legislation, approved after the extension of the parliamentary session, punishes the burning or damaging of the Japanese flag, including live broadcasts on social media showing the act.

title: Spain and Argentina contest the World Cup final with great global expectation
summary: The Spanish president attends the match amid a possible meeting with Donald Trump, while fans in Tokyo gather in Shibuya to experience the event.

title: Japanese government and Saga prefecture agree to start environmental assessment for new Shinkansen section
summary: The agreement allows progress on the environmental impact study of the western section of the high-speed line, without a defined route and with a cap on Saga's financial burden.

title: Hiroshima Carp infielder Masaya Yano suspended for meeting with an alleged illegal tobacco seller
summary: The team decided to remove Yano from the player register after the revelation that he had a private meeting with an individual linked to the trafficking of 'zombie' cigarettes.

title: Magnitude 7.3 earthquake in the Mexican Pacific generates tsunami alert in several countries
summary: The earthquake, which occurred off the coast of Mexico, caused tsunami warnings in Guatemala and other nations, but no serious damage was reported in Mexico.

title: United States intensifies attacks against Iran for sixth consecutive day and Tehran threatens retaliation
summary: The offensive expands to Iranian infrastructure, while the Revolutionary Guard launches an attack against US communication facilities in Syria, raising regional tensions.

title: Japan begins first review of its criminal case reopening system in decades
summary: A committee of experts presents proposals to streamline the processes of reviewing sentences, under pressure from victims of wrongful convictions seeking justice.

title: European Union softens emissions trading rules to ease pressure on companies
summary: The Brussels proposal slows down the pace of emission rights reduction, prioritizing industrial competitiveness over immediate climate goals.

title: Donald Trump reiterates allegations of Chinese interference in the 2020 election and demands electoral changes
summary: The former US president again denounced an alleged Chinese conspiracy, in addition to criticizing television networks that did not broadcast his speech live.

title: Hayato Sakamoto ties the Tokyo Giants' home run record with a three-run walk-off
summary: The veteran outfielder hit a home run in the ninth inning that gave the victory to his team, tying the franchise's historic record set by Sadaharu Oh.

title: Four Japanese artists designated as Living National Treasures
summary: The Ministry of Culture recognized masters of traditional music and metal craftsmanship as possessors of exceptional cultural skills.

title: Pianist and composer Takeshi Shibuya dies at 86
summary: Known for writing children's songs for the program 'Okaasan to Issho', Shibuya left a legacy in Japanese music and popular culture.

title: Voice actress Yukiko Nikaido, famous for playing Fujiko Mine in 'Lupin III', dies
summary: The renowned seiyū, wife of actor Hiroshi Yanagi, died at 87, remembered for her iconic character in the animated series.

title: Opposition party Reiwa Shinsengumi elects new leader among three candidates
summary: The internal election seeks to define the party's direction without the central figure of Taro Yamamoto, with debates about its ideological independence.

title: The controversial demonstration of the ABC conjecture by Shinichi Mochizuki remains without definitive validation
summary: A verification committee issued an interim report pointing out unclear aspects in the proof, prolonging the debate in the mathematical community.

title: Kyoto's Gion Festival holds its main parade with 23 floats under scorching heat
summary: Thousands of people watched the procession amid record temperatures, causing several cases of heatstroke among attendees.

title: Japanese lawmakers express concern over Trump's threat to dismantle the International Criminal Court
summary: A bipartisan group of the Diet issued a statement criticizing the US administration's plans to sanction the ICC, defending its role in global justice.
